differential_diagnoses: Adrenocortical carcinoma with metastatic central nervous system disease
next_steps: Send CSF for comprehensive infectious workup, including fungal, TB, and bacterial PCR/culture and cytology
Initiate empiric antifungal therapy (e.g., liposomal amphotericin B and flucytosine) and anti-tuberculosis therapy
Maintain broad-spectrum antibacterial coverage and consider acyclovir until HSV is ruled out
Perform repeat brain imaging, preferably MRI (with VNS-compatible protocols or specialized sequences to reduce artifact)
Coordinate endocrine consultation for urgent evaluation of hypercortisolism (Cushing's syndrome)
Review CSF markers for malignant cells, including flow cytometry for lymphoma
Monitor intracranial pressure and manage potential complications of elevated opening pressure

differential_diagnoses: Disseminated fungal meningitis (e.g., Cryptococcus or Histoplasma)
next_steps: Perform urgent serum endocrine workup including ACTH, morning cortisol, and plasma metanephrines
Send CSF for cryptococcal antigen, fungal culture, AFB culture, and PCR for TB and HSV
Perform CSF flow cytometry and repeat cytology to screen for lymphoma and metastatic carcinoma
Initiate broad-spectrum empiric antimicrobial coverage including anti-tuberculous and antifungal therapy
Consult neurosurgery for potential ICP management and neurology for urgent MRI with VNS-safe protocols
Perform repeat CT scan of chest/abdomen/pelvis to evaluate for metastatic disease progression
